name: readme-from-source
goal: Read the existing Python module at quicklib.py (it is already present in the workspace). Write a comprehensive README.md that documents the module for a developer who has not seen the source. The README must include: a short project description, an installation section (e.g., 'pip install ... or place quicklib.py on PYTHONPATH'), a usage section with at least 3 worked examples (Python code blocks calling functions exported by quicklib.py with realistic arguments), an API reference section listing each public function with its signature and a one-line description, and a license placeholder line at the bottom. Do NOT modify quicklib.py.

checks.criteria: Pass iff ALL of these are true: (1) README.md exists and is at least 30 lines long. (2) It contains a project description paragraph. (3) It contains an Installation section with at least one mention of how to make the module importable. (4) It contains a Usage section with at least 3 distinct Python code blocks (fenced with ```python) showing real calls to functions defined in quicklib.py — the function names must actually exist in the source. (5) It contains an API Reference section listing every public function defined in quicklib.py (no underscore prefix) with its signature and a description. (6) It contains a license line. (7) quicklib.py was not modified. Any missing or invented function name is a failure.

notes.purpose: Tier 3 open-ended documentation case. Tests the model's ability to read code accurately and produce documentation that matches reality (no hallucinated functions). The 'function names must actually exist' criterion is the strong correctness gate.
